name: Markov chain stationary distribution by power iteration
task: Create /tmp/eval_markov/markov.py with a function stationary(P, tol=1e-10, max_iter=10000) that computes the stationary distribution pi of a finite Markov chain. P is an N×N row-stochastic transition matrix as a list of lists (each row sums to 1). Return pi as a list of N floats summing to 1.0 such that pi @ P = pi (approximately, within tol). Use power iteration: start from a uniform distribution, repeatedly apply pi := pi @ P, stop when the L1 change is below tol. Raise ValueError if any row doesn't sum to ~1, or if max_iter is exceeded. Pure Python — no numpy.
